item.title: Six Takeaways From the Times Investigation Into Epstein’s Death
item.description: We considered every plausible theory of his death, both official and otherwise, seeking out the most persuasive arguments and evidence for each.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Feud With Delaware May Transform Corporate America
item.description: An angry Musk has taken his business elsewhere, and has urged other companies to follow suit.

item.title: My Nephew’s Comedy Routine Skewers His Grandma. Should the Adults Be Laughing?
item.description: My brother even shared a video of the bit in the family group chat.

item.title: How ‘Goy’ Went from Yiddish to Antisemitic Dog Whistle to Zoomer Slang
item.description: The word is toyed with at an amused and dismissive arm’s length, until it is miles from where it began.

item.title: Can a Dinner Host Set a Drink Limit?
item.description: A ruling on an alcohol-related dispute.

item.title: Our Allies Are Wondering Whether Supporting the American War Machine Is Worth It
item.description: Is the Iran war an early sign of “superpower suicide"?

item.title: Danny McBride Thinks Men Learned All the Wrong Lessons From Movies
item.description: The writer and actor, known for his profane comedic antiheroes, likes to find universal truths in human flaws.

item.title: I’m Worried My Friends’ Son May Harm Them. What Should I Do?
What I Learned About Masculinity at Thai Kickboxing School
item.description: A week inside a grueling Muay Thai training program in Phuket.

item.title: Of Course the Pope Is a Natural at Memes
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is the perfect vessel for 6-7 and other internet trends, where juxtaposition — and sparring with Trump — boost virality.

item.title: The Radical Pacifist Who Inspired the First Armed Rebellion of the Founding Era
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Native Leader Who Tied His People’s Fate to the Patriot Cause
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Many Founders of the United States
item.description: In the past 50 years, the way we tell the story of the Revolution has become dramatically more complex. Can it still inspire us all?

item.title: Visions of America: The Revolution as You’ve Never Seen It Before
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.
